data.title: PMB and Abrazo Health open 46,000 sq ft medical building and inpatient rehab facility in Goodyear, Arizona.
data.description: A healthcare facility spanning 46,000 square feet with 24 beds offers outpatient medical services for the community and inpatient rehabilitation in Goodyear, Arizona and San Diego. On January 30, 2026, PMB, a prominent healthcare real estate developer, partnered with Abrazo Health to establish this facility.

The report delves into recent market turbulence experienced by digital assets as investors reevaluate their expectations.

data.title: Inventor from Westland, Mich., invents new BUG B GONE to kill roaches, scorpions with one spray.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 30, 2026, an inventor from Westland, Michigan shared a new invention called BUG B GONE. The inventor explained the motivation behind creating this product as a solution for homeowners to eliminate roaches, scorpions, and other bugs with a single application. The innovative design aims to offer simplicity and effectiveness in pest control, providing a convenient solution for protecting homes.

data.title: Federal Signal (NYSE: FSS) to announce Q4 earnings on Feb 25, 2026. Leading in safety solutions.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, in Downers Grove, Illinois, Federal Signal Corporation (NYSE: FSS), a renowned provider of environmental and safety solutions, is set to reveal its fourth-quarter earnings on February 25, 2026, before the market opens. This update coincides with the Company's plans to conduct an investor briefing.

data.title: PMMI launches SMB FastTrack Program to support small and medium-sized manufacturers with tailored resources at PACK EXPO East 2026.
data.description: A newly launched initiative, PACK EXPO East 2026 aims to simplify small and medium-sized businesses’ (SMBs) access to technology, education, and business resources. The program, named SMB FastTrack, is tailored to SMBs and was unveiled in Herndon, Va., on Jan. 30, 2026. This program is geared towards aiding SMBs efficiently in locating solutions and resources beneficial to their growth.

data.description: In mid-October 2025, the IL Fund disclosed an extra withdrawal, marking the ultimate draw of all housing bonds. This payment is slated for December 15, 2025.

data.title: Paradex confirms end of XP Season 2, preps for $DIME TGE. Expands airdrop post-season, aims for alignment.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, in Singapore, Paradex declared the end of XP Season 2, signifying a significant achievement in the platform's journey towards the imminent $DIME Token Generation Event (TGE). This move demonstrates Paradex's dedication to prioritizing traders and ensuring their incentives are aligned. The TGE is a crucial step in the platform's roadmap, emphasizing the company's focus on providing a trader-centric environment.

data.title: Jury awards $30.5M against Seattle in Antonio Mays Jr. case; city found liable for teen's death during 2020 protests.
data.description: Landmark Decision Holds City Responsible for Death of 16-Year-Old Antonio Mays Jr. During 2020 CHOP Protests. On January 30, 2026, a jury in King County ruled that the City of Seattle is responsible for the tragic death of Antonio Mays Jr., a 16-year-old who was fatally shot on June 29. This verdict comes after a legal battle and marks a significant moment in the case, highlighting accountability for the loss of a young life. The decision signals a shift towards justice in this high-profile incident that occurred during the protests in the Capitol Hill Organized Protest (CHOP) zone in 2020. Antonio Mays Jr.'s death sparked outrage and calls for justice, leading to this verdict that holds the city liable for the wrongful death. This ruling sheds light on the events surrounding the shooting and underscores the importance of upholding accountability, bringing some closure to the family and community affected by the tragic incident.

data.title: National Press Club alarmed by journalist Don Lemon's arrest by federal agents in Los Angeles while covering the Grammy, Jan 30, 2026.
data.description: The President of the National Press Club, Mark Schoeff, Jr., expressed serious concern about the recent arrest of journalist Don Lemon in Los Angeles. Lemon was detained by federal agents on Thursday night while reporting on the Grammy event. This incident has raised significant alarm within the press community.

data.title: By 2030, 4D radar's share in global automotive industry will exceed 50% from being "optional." - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: A report on the Automotive 4D Radar Industry Research indicates that the market is rapidly growing, with 4D radar projected to account for over 50% share by 2030. This technology has evolved from being considered optional to becoming essential in vehicle sensors. 4D radar offers advanced capabilities such as detecting and analyzing object height data, as well as perceiving distance, speed, azimuth, and altitude. It is also immune to weather and lighting conditions, making it a crucial sensor for vehicles. This report has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings.

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive beats Q4 expectations with sales of $5.230 billion, 5.8% increase. Toothpaste market share at 41.3%.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE: CL) exceeded expectations, with adjusted earnings per share reaching 95 cents compared to the anticipated 91 cents. The company reported quarterly sales of $5.230 billion, surpassing the projected $5.118 billion, marking a 5.8% increase in net sales. Organic sales saw a 2.2% rise, despite a 0.9% decline from lower private-label pet volume. Colgate continued to lead the global toothpaste market with a 41.3% year-to-date share and retained the top position in manual toothbrushes at 32.4% for the year. The company's strong performance was driven by solid execution in oral care and pet nutrition, allowing it to withstand challenges in the global market.
